# Middle-earth Lore

## Characters

### Aragorn
Aragorn, son of Arathorn, is the heir to the throne of Gondor. He was raised in Rivendell by Elrond after his father's death. As a Ranger of the North, he protected the Shire and surrounding lands from various dangers. He is also known by his Ranger name, Strider.

### Gandalf
Gandalf the Grey is one of the Istari, wizards sent to Middle-earth to aid in the fight against Sauron. He is known for his wisdom, magical abilities, and his trademark fireworks. He wields the sword Glamdring and later becomes Gandalf the White.

### Frodo Baggins
Frodo is a hobbit from the Shire who inherits the One Ring from his uncle Bilbo. He undertakes the dangerous quest to destroy the Ring in the fires of Mount Doom, accompanied by his faithful friend Sam Gamgee.

## Locations

### Rivendell
Rivendell, also known as Imladris, is an elven refuge in Middle-earth. It is the home of Elrond Half-elven and serves as a sanctuary for the Free Peoples. The Council of Elrond was held here, where the Fellowship of the Ring was formed.

### The Shire
The Shire is the homeland of the hobbits in the northwest of Middle-earth. It is a peaceful, agrarian land divided into four Farthings. Hobbits are generally uninterested in the affairs of the wider world, preferring comfort and routine.

### Gondor
Gondor is the greatest kingdom of Men in Middle-earth during the Third Age. Its capital is Minas Tirith, a multi-tiered city built into the side of Mount Mindolluin. Gondor has long stood as a bulwark against the forces of Mordor.

## Items

### The One Ring
The One Ring is the most powerful of the Rings of Power, forged by the Dark Lord Sauron to control all other rings. It grants invisibility to mortal wearers and extends their lifespan unnaturally. The Ring exerts a corrupting influence on those who possess it.

### Andúril
Andúril, the Flame of the West, is the sword wielded by Aragorn. It was reforged from the shards of Narsil, the sword that cut the One Ring from Sauron's hand. The sword was reforged in Rivendell as a symbol of Aragorn's claim to Gondor's throne.

## Events

### The Council of Elrond
The Council of Elrond was a meeting held in Rivendell to decide the fate of the One Ring. Representatives of the Free Peoples attended, including Elves, Dwarves, Men, and Hobbits. It was decided that the Ring must be destroyed in Mount Doom.

### The Battle of Helm's Deep
The Battle of Helm's Deep was a major engagement between the forces of Rohan and Saruman's army of Uruk-hai. The battle took place at the fortress of Helm's Deep in Rohan. Despite being outnumbered, the Rohirrim were victorious with the aid of Gandalf and the Huorns.

## Concepts

### The Fellowship of the Ring
The Fellowship of the Ring was formed at the Council of Elrond with the mission to destroy the One Ring. It consisted of nine members: four hobbits (Frodo, Sam, Merry, Pippin), two men (Aragorn, Boromir), one elf (Legolas), one dwarf (Gimli), and one wizard (Gandalf).

### The Third Age
The Third Age is the period of Middle-earth's history during which most of the events of The Lord of the Rings take place. It began after the defeat of Sauron at the end of the Second Age and ended with the destruction of the One Ring and Sauron's final defeat.
